---
title: "Proof Title"
description: "What claim is proved and why the proof matters."
page-type: proof
status: draft
update-status: major-refresh
level: foundation
topic: replace-me
prerequisites:
  - /topics/foundation/replace-me/index.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "State the claim with assumptions."
  - "Explain the proof strategy before the details."
  - "Extract one reusable proof pattern."
applications:
  - replace-me
keywords:
  - proof
  - replace-me
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-24
source-check-date: 2026-04-24
review-cadence: annual
date-modified: last-modified
draft: true
---

<!--
Before writing:
1. Verify theorem statement and assumptions from a primary source
2. Decide whether this page teaches a full proof or a proof pattern
3. Make sure all dependencies are already available somewhere on the site
4. Update freshness fields after checking sources
-->

## Claim

State the result exactly.

::: {#thm-proof-page}
## Theorem

Write the theorem, proposition, or lemma with assumptions.
:::

## Why This Proof Matters

Explain why this proof is worth learning and what later topics reuse it.

## Dependencies

- definition or theorem 1
- definition or theorem 2

## Strategy Before Details

Name the method first: direct proof, contradiction, induction, exchange argument, averaging, compactness, convexity, spectral argument, and so on.

## Full Proof

::: {.proof}
Write the proof here.
:::

## Step Annotations

Break down the nontrivial steps.

1. step and why it is valid
2. step and why it is valid
3. step and why it is valid

## Why The Assumptions Matter

Explain which assumptions are essential and what can fail without them.

## Common Failure Modes

- hidden assumption
- invalid inference
- notation mistake

## Reusable Pattern

Extract the general proof move the reader should carry forward.

## Where This Shows Up Again

Name later topics, algorithms, or papers where the same style appears.

## Exercises

1. identify the missing step
2. adapt the proof to a nearby statement
3. test what happens when an assumption is removed

## Sources and Further Reading

- [Source title](https://example.com) - `First pass` - why it is included
- [Source title](https://example.com) - `Paper bridge` - why it is included
